description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped standing stones, each with a weathered, gray surface mottled with patches of green moss or lichen. The largest stone stands upright in the center, flanked by two smaller stones, one of which leans slightly to the side. The front faces of the stones feature shallow, carved symbols or runes, giving them an ancient or mystical appearance. The surfaces are rough and uneven, with visible pitting and natural erosion, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. Subtle color variations and organic growths enhance the sense of age and integration with nature. The overall grouping suggests a deliberate arrangement, possibly as a decorative or symbolic barrier in an urban park or garden.
description: A trio of weathered, mossy gray stones with carved runes, arranged as a naturalistic barrier. The stones show rough, aged surfaces and subtle green patches.
description_view_0: Three stones with visible carved runes and prominent green moss patches, largest stone in the center.
description_view_1: Rear view showing the tall stone's back, with less visible carvings and more pronounced surface pitting.
description_view_2: Back side of the stones, carvings are faint or absent, and the moss coverage is more evenly distributed.
description_view_3: Side view highlighting the height of the central stone and the rounded forms of the flanking stones.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or decorative barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Shallow carved runes on the front faces, irregular mottling from moss and lichen, and natural pitting from erosion.
attribute_weathering: Rough with visible moss and lichen, pitted and eroded surfaces indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with planted or grassy areas, partially covered by moss and lichen, visually harmonizing with natural landscaping.
